speaker: Philip Lane
text:
The economy grew by 5.2% in the latest quarter, slightly above market expectations. Business investment has recovered, supported by public sector spending. The services sector continues to expand steadily, while manufacturing output has stabilised.

Household consumption remains subdued, reflecting the balance between real income growth and the drag from higher borrowing costs. Consumer confidence has deteriorated in recent months. The housing market shows signs of continued adjustment.

title: Setting policy in uncertain times
speaker: Dave Ramsden
text:
The transmission of our previous rate increases to the real economy is still working through. Mortgage rates have stabilised, and credit conditions have remained tight. The full impact of cumulative tightening is expected to materialise over the coming quarters.

title: The inflation outlook: challenges and opportunities
speaker: Committee
text:
Our projections indicate that inflation will remain elevated before easing over the medium term, converging towards our 2% target by the end of 2026. However, risks to this forecast are tilted to the upside, reflecting uncertainty around global commodity markets and domestic wage dynamics.

We continue to monitor inflation expectations closely, as their anchoring is critical for the credibility of our monetary policy framework.
